title: Number not divisible by 2 or 3 or 5

content.problemStatement: Given an integer **N**, print the number of elements between 111 and **N** that are **not divisible** by 222, 333, or 555.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the count of integers i such that 111 \u2264 i \u2264 N and i is not divisible by 222, 333 or 555.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All numbers from 111 to 120 are smaller than 222, 333 and 555, so none is divisible by any of them. The interval length is 120‑111+1 = 10.
150 is still below the three divisors, therefore every integer in [111,150] satisfies the condition. Count = 150‑111+1 = 40.
200 < 222,333,555, so all numbers in the interval are valid. Count = 200‑111+1 = 90.

solution.approach:
For a given N we first count all integers in the interval [111, N] (if N < 111 the answer is 0). Then we subtract the numbers that are divisible by at least one of 222, 333 or 555. Using the inclusion‑exclusion principle we compute:
  cnt222 = floor(N/222), cnt333 = floor(N/333), cnt555 = floor(N/555)
  cntLCM22_33 = floor(N/lcm(222,333)) = floor(N/666)
  cntLCM22_55 = floor(N/lcm(222,555)) = floor(N/1110)
  cntLCM33_55 = floor(N/lcm(333,555)) = floor(N/1665)
  cntAll = floor(N/lcm(222,333,555)) = floor(N/3330)
  union = cnt222 + cnt333 + cnt555 - (cntLCM22_33 + cntLCM22_55 + cntLCM33_55) + cntAll
Answer = totalInRange - union.
All operations are O(1) per test case, so the whole solution runs in O(T) time with O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) overall, O(1) per test case.
